generatedSentence: A nervous junior wanderer, a nascent wizard, felt an uncomfortable tension in the chrome hallway of the solar observatory. Despite the futuristic environment with its humming trijets and glowing orbs of diode light, he couldn't afford the luxury of nonchalance. He would hide his fragile hippy interaction during the long workweek, clutching a silver thermos and silverware, wondering if his true intentions were too exposed. His secret prayer was for restoration, to remove the oppressive oligarchy that flushed the planetary artery dry. The unbelievable revolution had begun, a runaway force of machinery. He intended to lead a distaff rebellion. He felt naughty for even thinking of jacking their systems, the weight of ages, of epochs, resting upon him, a cowardly goblin’s grin on his bobblehead companion. He knew this was really the final matchup. The cyclops-like central computer began to bombard their position, its unitary effects thwarting their every move. The team, now freaked and numbing, prepared for an astonishing final push. A warrior, undefeated, voyaged on pickups through a ravaged vineyard, carrying meager rations of apricots. His every movement was a flicker, like a cricket, as he attempted to rewind the train of destruction, its tracks now bricked. Through the telescope, he watched as justice, a jester drumming for freedom, finally empowered the citizenry. The revolution would unravel the oligarchy's perfection. Grandparents recounted fanfics of the fight, their memories washed of hemlock's aftertaste. Firefighters, smelling fragrances of newly bloomed honeycomb, used forceps to restore the fried machinery. Luxuries and leisure returned, no more hungry bonkers jewfish, just katydids in khakis singing karaoke.

He would hide his fragile hippy interaction during the long workweek, clutching a silver thermos and silverware, wondering if his true intentions were too exposed.

His secret prayer was for restoration, to remove the oppressive oligarchy that flushed the planetary artery dry. The unbelievable revolution had begun, a runaway force of machinery. The cyclops-like central computer began to bombard their position, its unitary effects thwarting their every move. The team, now freaked and numbing, prepared for an astonishing final push. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A young, slender wizard-engineer wearing a patched, tech-infused brown trench coat and glowing blue goggles. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Young wizard-engineer nervously adjusts glowing goggles in a sleek, silver hallway with glowing blue diode orbs, a faint reflection of trijets visible outside a window, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The wizard-engineer, now determined, leads a small, diverse distaff rebellion against towering, oppressive dark machinery, a wide shot from a slightly low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A lone, rugged warrior on a futuristic pickup truck navigates through a post-apocalyptic vineyard, avoiding bombarding energy blasts from a monolithic cyclops-like structure in the distance, dramatic high-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The wizard-engineer and team celebrate with liberated citizens in a restored, lush urban landscape, a jester figure drumming nearby, a wide shot showcasing communal joy, warm golden hour lighting.

definitions.definition: The situation or occurrence in which two or more objects or events act upon one another to produce a new effect; the effect resulting from such a situation or occurrence.
definitions.example: Be aware of interactions between different medications.

definitions.definition: A conversation or exchange between people.
definitions.example: I enjoyed the interaction with a bunch of like-minded people.

definitions.definition: An aircraft powered by three jet engines.
Three jets of particles from an event or source
Something that causes amazement or awe; a marvel.
definitions.example: Wonders of the World seem to come in sevens.

definitions.definition: Something astonishing and seemingly inexplicable.
definitions.example: The idea was so crazy that it is a wonder that anyone went along with it.

definitions.definition: Someone very talented at something, a genius.
definitions.example: He's a wonder at cooking.

definitions.definition: The sense or emotion which can be inspired by something curious or unknown; surprise; astonishment, often with awe or reverence.
A mental pondering, a thought.
A kind of donut; a cruller.
To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.
To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
definitions.example: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

definitions.definition: One who wanders, who travels aimlessly.
Any of various far-migrating nymphalid butterflies of the genus Danaus.
The wandering albatross, Diomedea exulans.
A practice of communicating with one's God.
definitions.example: Through prayer I ask for God's blessings.

definitions.definition: The act of praying.
definitions.example: In many cultures, prayer involves singing.

definitions.definition: The specific words or methods used for praying.
definitions.example: Christians recite the Lord's Prayer.

definitions.definition: A meeting held for the express purpose of praying.
definitions.example: Grandpa never misses a chance to go to prayer.

definitions.definition: A request; a petition.
definitions.example: This, your honor, is my prayer; that all here be set free.

definitions.definition: (mostly in negative constructions) The remotest hope or chance.
definitions.example: That team doesn't have a prayer of winning the championship.

definitions.definition: Never defeated; always victorious
definitions.example: He was the undefeated tiddlywinks champion of the school.

definitions.definition: The process of bringing an object back to its original state; the process of restoring something.
definitions.example: The restoration of this medieval church involved undoing all the Victorian modifications.

definitions.definition: The return of a former monarchy or monarch to power, usually after having been forced to step down.
definitions.example: The restoration of the House of Stuart took place a few years after the death of Cromwell.

definitions.definition: The receiving of a sinner to divine favor.
The act of removing something.
(archaic) Removing a dish at a meal in order to replace it with the next course, a dish thus replaced, or the replacement.
(at some public schools) A division of the school, especially the form prior to last
A step or gradation (as in the phrase "at one remove")
Distance in time or space; interval.
(by extension) Emotional distance or indifference.
The transfer of one's home or business to another place; a move.
The act of resetting a horse's shoe.
To move something from one place to another, especially to take away.
definitions.example: He removed the marbles from the bag.

definitions.definition: To murder.
To dismiss a batsman.
To discard, set aside, especially something abstract (a thought, feeling, etc.).
To depart, leave.
To change one's residence; to move.
To dismiss or discharge from office.
definitions.example: The President removed many postmasters.

definitions.definition: A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
The removal and replacement of a government, especially by sudden violent action.
Rotation: the turning of an object around an axis.
A rotation: one complete turn of an object during rotation.
In the case of celestial bodies - the traversal of one body through an orbit around another body.
A sudden, vast change in a situation, a discipline, or the way of thinking and behaving.
A round of periodic changes, such as between the seasons of the year.
Consideration of an idea; the act of revolving something in the mind.
A corridor in a building that connects rooms.
To cause to take flight from concealment.
definitions.example: The hunters flushed the tiger from the canebrake.

definitions.definition: To take suddenly to flight, especially from cover.
definitions.example: A covey of quail flushed from the undergrowth.

definitions.definition: To cleanse by flooding with generous quantities of a fluid.
definitions.example: Flush the injury with plenty of water.

definitions.definition: Particularly, to cleanse a toilet by introducing a large amount of water.
To become suffused with reddish color due to embarrassment, excitement, overheating, or other systemic disturbance, to blush.
definitions.example: The damsel flushed at the scoundrel's suggestion.

definitions.definition: To cause to blush.
To cause to be full; to flood; to overflow; to overwhelm with water.
definitions.example: to flush the meadows

definitions.definition: To excite, inflame.
(of a toilet) To be cleansed by being flooded with generous quantities of water.
definitions.example: There must be somebody home: I just heard the toilet flushing.

definitions.definition: To clear (a buffer) of its contents.
To flow and spread suddenly; to rush.
definitions.example: Blood flushes into the face.

definitions.definition: To show red; to shine suddenly; to glow.
To fill in (joints); to point the level; to make them flush.
To operate a placer mine, where the continuous supply of water is insufficient, by holding back the water, and releasing it periodically in a flood.
To fill underground spaces, especially in coal mines, with material carried by water, which, after drainage, constitutes a compact mass.
To dispose or be disposed of by flushing down a toilet
Red in the face because of embarrassment, exertion, etc.
An efferent blood vessel from the heart, conveying blood away from the heart regardless of oxygenation status; see pulmonary artery.
A major transit corridor.
To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;—with an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.
definitions.example: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.  We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.

definitions.definition: To offer, provide, or supply, as in selling, granting, expending, with profit, or without loss or too great injury.
definitions.example: A affords his goods cheaper than B.

definitions.definition: To give forth; to supply, yield, or produce as the natural result, fruit, or issue.
definitions.example: Grapes afford wine.  Olives afford oil.  The earth affords fruit.  The sea affords an abundant supply of fish.

definitions.definition: To give, grant, or confer, with a remoter reference to its being the natural result; to provide; to furnish.
definitions.example: A good life affords consolation in old age.

definitions.definition: (usually followed by the particle "to") To hope; to wish (something, or something to be accomplished); be intent upon
definitions.example: He intends to go to university.

definitions.definition: To fix the mind on; attend to; take care of; superintend; regard.
To stretch to extend; distend.
To strain; make tense.
To intensify; strengthen.
To apply with energy.
To bend or turn; direct, as one’s course or journey.
To design mechanically or artistically; fashion; mold.
To pretend; counterfeit; simulate.
To raise using a jack.
definitions.example: He jacked the car up so that he could replace the brake pads.

definitions.definition: To raise or increase.
definitions.example: If you want to jack your stats you just write off failures as invalid results.

definitions.definition: To produce by freeze distillation; to distil (an alcoholic beverage) by freezing it and removing the ice (which is water), leaving the alcohol (which remains liquid).
To steal something, typically an automobile. Shortened form of carjacking.
definitions.example: Someone jacked my car last night!

definitions.definition: To dance by moving the torso forward and backward in a rippling motion.
To hit (the ball) hard; especially, to hit (the ball) out of the field, producing a home run.
A street robbery.
Indifference; carelessness; coolness; disregard, detachment.
Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
definitions.example: Some naughty boys at school hid the teacher's lesson notes.

definitions.definition: Sexually provocative; now in weakened sense, risqué, cheeky.
definitions.example: I bought some naughty lingerie for my honeymoon.

definitions.definition: Evil, wicked, morally reprehensible.
Bad, worthless, substandard.
A spherical body; a globe; especially, one of the celestial spheres; a sun, planet, or star
One of the azure transparent spheres conceived by the ancients to be enclosed one within another, and to carry the heavenly bodies in their revolutions
A circle; especially, a circle, or nearly circular orbit, described by the revolution of a heavenly body; an orbit
A period of time marked off by the revolution of a heavenly body.
The eye, as luminous and spherical
A revolving circular body; a wheel
A sphere of action.
A globus cruciger; a ceremonial sphere used to represent royal power
A translucent sphere appearing in flash photography (Orb (optics))
A body of soldiers drawn up in a circle, as for defence, especially infantry to repel cavalry.
A blank window or panel.
An electronic device that allows current to flow in one direction only; used chiefly as a rectifier.
A bottle, flask or similar vessel having a vacuum between its inner and outer silvered walls; designed to maintain the temperature of its contents
Anything made from silver.
Anything with a silvery colour.
Knives, forks and spoons.
Trophies, success in a competition.
A lustrous, white, metallic element, atomic number 47, atomic weight 107.87, symbol Ag.
(collectively) Coins made from silver or any similar white metal.
(collectively) Cutlery and other eating utensils, whether silver or made from some other white metal.
(collectively) Any items made from silver or any other white metal.
A shiny gray color.
Anything resembling silver; something shiny and white.
To acquire a silvery colour.
To cover with silver, or with a silvery metal.
definitions.example: to silver a pin;  to silver a glass mirror plate with an amalgam of tin and mercury

definitions.definition: To polish like silver; to impart a brightness to, like that of silver.
To make hoary, or white, like silver.
Made from silver.
Made from another white metal.
Having a color like silver: a shiny gray.
Denoting the twenty-fifth anniversary, especially of a wedding.
(of commercial services) Premium, but inferior to gold.
Having the clear, musical tone of silver; soft and clear in sound.
definitions.example: a silver-voiced young girl

definitions.definition: The range of days of the week that are normally worked
definitions.example: A 32-hour workweek is a 4-day week and an 8-hour day combined.

definitions.definition: Not comfortable; causing discomfort.
definitions.example: The class squirmed and fidgeted in the uncomfortable new chairs.

definitions.definition: Experiencing discomfort.
Uneasy or anxious.
definitions.example: Sharing a house with them made me uncomfortable.

definitions.definition: Put off or disgusted.
The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
The natural world or ecosystem.
All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.
The software and/or hardware existing on any particular computer system.
definitions.example: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.

definitions.definition: The environment of a function at a point during the execution of a program is the set of identifiers in the function's scope and their bindings at that point.
The set of variables and their values in a namespace that an operating system associates with a process.
The work involved in performing an activity; exertion.
definitions.example: It took a lot of effort to find a decent-sized, fully-furnished apartment within walking distance of the office.   He made a conscious effort to not appear affected by the stories in the paper.

definitions.definition: An endeavour.
definitions.example: Although he didn't win any medals, Johnson's effort at the Olympics won over many fans.

definitions.definition: A force acting on a body in the direction of its motion.
To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.
definitions.example: He hides his magazines under the bed.

definitions.definition: To put oneself in a place where one will be harder to find or out of sight.
(1950s slang) A teenager who imitated the beatniks.

(1960s slang; still widely used in reference to that era) One who chooses not to conform to prevailing social norms: especially one who subscribes to values or actions such as acceptance or self-practice of recreational drug use, liberal or radical sexual mores, advocacy of communal living, strong pacifism or anti-war sentiment, etc.
(modern slang) A person who keeps an unkempt or sloppy appearance and wearing unusually long hair (for males), and because of it, often stereotyped as a deadbeat.
Someone who dresses in a hippie style.
One who is hip.
Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.
definitions.example: He is a very fragile person and gets easily depressed.

definitions.definition: Feeling weak or easily disturbed as a result of illness.
A collectible doll with a bobbing head
A younger person.
definitions.example: four years his junior

definitions.definition: A name suffix used after a son's name when his father has the same name (abbreviations: Jnr., Jr., Jun.).
A third-year student at a high school or university.
A junior barrister.
Low in rank; having a subordinate role, job, or situation.
(often preceded by a possessive adjective or a possessive form of a noun) Younger.
Belonging to a younger person, or an earlier time of life.
Of or pertaining to a third academic year in a four-year high school (eleventh grade) or university.
Of sinews and tendons.
Of nerves.
The time of noon; the time of day when the sun is highest in the sky, especially on a hot day.
The time of greatest prosperity.
Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun
definitions.example: solar influence

definitions.definition: Born under the predominant influence of the sun.
Measured by the progress or revolution of the sun in the ecliptic; as, the solar year.
Produced by the action of the sun, or peculiarly affected by its influence.
(physical) Matter, material.
A large quantity; a sum.
The Eucharist, now especially in Roman Catholicism.
Celebration of the Eucharist.
(usually as the Mass) The sacrament of the Eucharist.
A musical setting of parts of the mass.
The force on an object due to the gravitational attraction between it and the Earth (or whatever astronomical object it is primarily influenced by).
An object used to make something heavier.
A standardized block of metal used in a balance to measure the mass of another object.
Importance or influence.
An object, such as a weight plate or barbell, used for strength training.
definitions.example: He's working out with weights.

definitions.definition: (lubricants) viscosity rating.
Mass (atomic weight, molecular weight, etc.) (in restricted circumstances)
(measurement) Mass (net weight, troy weight, carat weight, etc.).
A variable which multiplies a value for ease of statistical manipulation.
The smallest cardinality of a base.
The boldness of a font; the relative thickness of its strokes.
(visual art) The relative thickness of a drawn rule or painted brushstroke, line weight.
(visual art) The illusion of mass.
(visual art) The thickness and opacity of paint.
Pressure; burden.
definitions.example: the weight of care or business

definitions.definition: The resistance against which a machine acts, as opposed to the power which moves it.
Shipments of (often illegal) drugs.
definitions.example: He was pushing weight.

definitions.definition: One pound (1 lb) of drugs, especially cannabis.
To add weight to something; to make something heavier.
To load, burden or oppress someone.
To assign weights to individual statistics.
To bias something; to slant.
To handicap a horse with a specified weight.
To give a certain amount of force to a throw, kick, hit, etc.
One of various hostile supernatural creatures, now especially (fantasy literature) a malevolent and grotesque diminutive humanoid.
A particular period of history, especially one considered noteworthy or remarkable.
A notable event which marks the beginning of such a period.
A precise instant of time that is used as a point of reference.
Uses in computing.
A unit of geologic time subdividing a period into smaller parts.
A person or animal that runs away or has run away; a person, animal, or organization that escapes captivity or restrictions.
definitions.example: Runaway children are vulnerable to criminal exploitation.

definitions.definition: A vehicle (especially, a train) that is out of control.
(usually attributive) An object or process that is out of control or out of equilibrium.
The act of running away, especially of a horse or teams.
An overwhelming victory.
definitions.example: The home side won in a runaway.

definitions.definition: Having run away; escaped; fugitive
definitions.example: a runaway thief

definitions.definition: Easily won, as a contest
definitions.example: a runaway victory at the polls

definitions.definition: Unchecked; rampant
definitions.example: runaway prices

definitions.definition: Deserting or revolting against one's group, duties, expected conduct, or the like, especially to establish or join a rival group, change one's life drastically, etc.
definitions.example: The runaway delegates nominated their own candidate.

definitions.definition: A medieval primitive cannon, used chiefly in sieges for throwing heavy stone balls.
A bassoon-like medieval instrument
A large liquor container made of leather, in the form of a jug or a bottle.
A bombardment.
A bombardon.
A voluntary gift or contribution for a specific cause.
definitions.example: They were collecting donations for the elderly at Christmas.

definitions.definition: The act of giving or bestowing; a grant.

A device to which a bundle of natural fibres (often wool, flax, or cotton) are attached for temporary storage, before being drawn off gradually to spin thread. A traditional distaff is a staff with flax fibres tied loosely to it (as indicated by the etymology of the word), but modern distaffs are often made of cords weighted with beads, and attached to the wrist.
The part of a spinning wheel from which fibre is drawn to be spun.
Anything traditionally done by or considered of importance to women only.
A race for female horses only.
A woman, or women considered as a group.
Of, relating to, or characteristic of women.
Of the maternal side of a family.
Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
One who is especially skilled or unusually talented in a particular field.
definitions.example: He was a financial wizard, capable of predicting the movements of the stock markets.

definitions.definition: A computer program or script used to simplify complex operations, often for an inexperienced user.
definitions.example: Use the "Add Network Connection" wizard to connect to a network in a series of simple steps.

definitions.definition: One of the administrators of a multi-user dungeon.
A wise man; a sage.
An adult virgin over the age of 30.
To practice wizardry.
To conjure.
Fine, superb (originally RAF slang).
A pairing of two things, people or teams, especially for a competition
The quality or state of being perfect or complete, so that nothing substandard remains; the highest attainable state or degree of excellence
definitions.example: The system runs to perfection.

definitions.definition: A quality, endowment, or acquirement completely excellent; an ideal; faultlessness; especially, the divine attribute of complete excellence.
To perfect.
To separate the threads (of); disentangle.
definitions.example: Mother couldn't unravel the ball of wool after the cat had played with it.

definitions.definition: (of threads, etc.) To become separated; (of something woven, knitted, etc.) to come apart.
To clear from complication or difficulty; to unfold; to solve.
definitions.example: to unravel a mystery

definitions.definition: To separate the connected or united parts of; to throw into disorder; to confuse.
definitions.example: to unravel the broad consensus which was created

definitions.definition: To become undone; to collapse.
Not to be believed.
Incredible; so surprising it is almost unable to believe.
definitions.example: The most unbelievable thing happened to me today!

definitions.definition: Implausible or improbable.
definitions.example: His excuse seems rather unbelievable.

definitions.definition: A heavy, pliable, inelastic metal element, having a bright, bluish color, but easily tarnished; both malleable and ductile, though with little tenacity. It is easily fusible, forms alloys with other metals, and is an ingredient of solder and type metal. Atomic number 82, symbol Pb (from Latin plumbum).
A plummet or mass of lead attached to a line, used in sounding depth at sea or to estimate velocity in knots.
A thin strip of type metal, used to separate lines of type in printing.
Vertical space in advance of a row or between rows of text. Also known as leading.
definitions.example: This copy has too much lead; I prefer less space between the lines.

definitions.definition: Sheets or plates of lead used as a covering for roofs.
(plural leads) A roof covered with lead sheets or terne plates.
A thin cylinder of black lead or plumbago (graphite) used in pencils.
Bullets; ammunition.
definitions.example: They pumped him full of lead.

definitions.definition: To cover, fill, or affect with lead
definitions.example: continuous firing leads the grooves of a rifle.

definitions.definition: To place leads between the lines of.
definitions.example: leaded matter

definitions.definition: To pack again.
To clean the bearings and replace the grease on a wheel.
In a way or manner that is real, not unreal.
(modal) Actually; in fact; in reality.
definitions.example: "He really is a true friend." / "Really? What makes you so sure?"

definitions.definition: (as an intensifier) Very (modifying an adjective); very much (modifying a verb).
definitions.example: But ma, I really, really want to go to the show!

definitions.definition: Indicating surprise at, or requesting confirmation of, some new information; to express skepticism.
definitions.example: A: He won the Nobel Prize yesterday.

definitions.definition: (sarcastic, typically exaggerated question.) Indicating that what was just said was obvious and unnecessary; contrived incredulity
definitions.example: A: I've just been reading Shakespeare - he's one of the best authors like, ever!

definitions.definition: Indicating affirmation, agreement.
definitions.example: A: That girl talks about herself way too much.

definitions.definition: Indicating displeasure at another person's behaviour or statement.
definitions.example: Well, really! How rude.

definitions.definition: A portion of some limited resource allocated to a person or group.
To supply with a ration; to limit (someone) to a specific allowance of something.
definitions.example: We rationed ourselves to three sips of water a day until we were rescued.

definitions.definition: To portion out (especially during a shortage of supply); to limit access to.
definitions.example: By the third day on the raft, we had to ration our water.

definitions.definition: To restrict (an activity etc.)
definitions.example: Our present health care system is rationed only to those who can afford it because of unnecessary high cost, lack of insurance coverage by 47 million people, and exorbitant prescription prices.

definitions.definition: A round sweet and juicy stone fruit, resembling peach or plum in taste, with a yellow-orange flesh, lightly fuzzy skin and a large seed inside.
The apricot tree, Prunus armeniaca
A pale yellow-orange colour, like that of an apricot fruit.
A dog with an orange-coloured coat.
The junction of the brain and brain stem on a target, used as an aiming point to ensure a one-shot kill.
(usually in the plural) A testicle.
A taste of something that persists when it is no longer present.
The persistence of the taste of something no longer present.
definitions.example: Many people think certain artificial sweeteners have an unpleasant aftertaste.

definitions.definition: Of wine, finish.

A form of entertainment popular in clubs, at parties, etc, in which individual members of the public sing along to pre-recorded instrumental versions of popular songs, the lyrics of which are displayed for the singer on a screen in time with the music.
A karaoke session.
To perform karaoke
A relative of grasshoppers and crickets, in the family Tettigoniidae.
Khaki-coloured/colored cotton trousers (pants).
A khaki uniform of pants (trousers) or shorts, shirt, and hat or other head covering such as a turban.
The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
definitions.example: the justice of a description

definitions.definition: The ideal of fairness, impartiality, etc., especially with regard to the punishment of wrongdoing.
definitions.example: Justice was served.

definitions.definition: Judgment and punishment of a party who has allegedly wronged another.
definitions.example: to demand justice

definitions.definition: The civil power dealing with law.
definitions.example: Ministry of Justice

definitions.definition: A title given to judges of certain courts; capitalized as a title.
definitions.example: Mr. Justice Krever presides over the appellate court

definitions.definition: Correctness, conforming to reality or rules.
One who jests, jokes or teases.
A person in colourful garb and fool's cap who amused a medieval and early modern royal or noble court.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Southeast Asian genus Symbrenthia.
The act of beating a drum.
A noise resembling that of a drum being beaten.
In many species of catfish, the sound produced by contraction of specialized sonic muscles with subsequent reverberation through the swim bladder.
Showing cowardice; lacking in courage; weakly fearful.
In the manner of a coward, cowardlily.
A one-eyed giant from Greek and Roman mythology.
A one-eyed creature of any species.
A person with only one working eye.
Any copepod in the genus Cyclops
An insect in the order Orthoptera, especially family Gryllidae, that makes a chirping sound by rubbing its wing casings against combs on its hind legs.
A wooden footstool.
A signalling device used by soldiers in hostile territory to identify themselves to a friendly in low visibility conditions.
A relatively small area of a roof constructed to divert water from a horizontal intersection of the roof with a chimney, wall, expansion joint or other projection.
The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
The working parts of a machine as a group.
The collective parts of something which allow it to function.
The literary devices used in a work, notably for dramatic effect

One of the Scandinavian or other Northern European seafaring warriors that raided (and then settled) the British Isles and other parts of Europe in the 8th to the 11th centuries and, according to many historians, were the first Europeans to reach North America.
(by extension) A stock character common in the fantasy genre, namely a barbarian, generally equipped with an axe or sword and a helmet adorned with horns.
A Norseman (mediaeval Scandinavian).
(mildly) An ethnic Swede, Norwegian, Dane, Icelander or Faroe Islander.
A player on the Minnesota Vikings NFL team.
A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
definitions.example: The vineyard of Château Margaux stands as the producer of one of the world's greatest and most sought-after red wines.

definitions.definition: To go on a long journey.
A person who puts out fires.
A pleasant smell or odour.
An instrument used in surgery or medical procedures for grasping and holding objects, similar to tongs or pincers.
An unsteady flash of light.
definitions.example: the flicker of a poorly tuned television set

definitions.definition: A short moment.
To burn or shine unsteadily, or with a wavering light.
To keep going on and off; to appear and disappear for short moments; to flutter.
To flutter; to flap the wings without flying.
To make greatly distressed and/or a discomposed appearance
To be placed or place someone under the influence of a psychedelic drug
To streak; to variegate
To experience reality withdrawal, or hallucinations (nightmarish), to behave irrational or unconventional due to drug use.
To react extremely or irrationally, usually under distress or discomposure
Cooked by frying.
(specifically, of an egg) Fried with the yolk unbroken.
definitions.example: He always ate his eggs fried, never scrambled.

definitions.definition: Cooked in a deep fryer or pressure fryer or the like after being coated (breaded) in batter; compare deep-fried.
definitions.example: a bucket of fried chicken

definitions.definition: (of computer equipment) Broken as a result of excessive heat or an electrical surge.
definitions.example: It looks like your motherboard is fried.

definitions.definition: Stoned; under the influence of drugs
definitions.example: Man, I got totally fried on weed at Chad's party.'

definitions.definition: Extremely tired due to exertion or stress; exhausted
definitions.example: After nearly twelve hours at the office plus a nightmare commute home, I was fried and couldn’t think of anything but sleep.

definitions.definition: A method of cooking food.
To be affected by extreme heat or current.
Fan fiction
A work of fan fiction
To surprise greatly.
Causing astonishment.
Very recently; in the immediate past.
definitions.example: She smelled the newly budding flowers.

definitions.definition: (Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
A young girl, especially one who is attractive, beautiful or graceful.
The larva of certain insects.
Any of various butterflies of the family Nymphalidae.
A government run by only a few, often the wealthy.
Those who make up an oligarchic government.
A state ruled by such a government.
A place where stars, planets and other celestial bodies are observed, usually through a telescope; also place for observing meteorological or other natural phenomena.
A lookout (vantage point with a view of the surrounding area)
The use of "damn" as a curse.
definitions.example: He said a few damns and left.

definitions.definition: A small, negligible quantity, being of little value; a whit or jot.
definitions.example: The new hires aren't worth a damn.

definitions.definition: The smallest amount of concern or consideration.
definitions.example: I don't give a damn.

definitions.definition: To condemn to hell.
definitions.example: I damn you eternally, fiend!

definitions.definition: To condemn; to declare guilty; to doom; to adjudge to punishment.
To put out of favor; to ruin; to label negatively.
definitions.example: I’m afraid that if I speak out on this, I’ll be damned as a troublemaker.

definitions.definition: To condemn as unfit, harmful, invalid, immoral or illegal.
To curse; put a curse upon.
definitions.example: That man stole my wallet. Damn him!

definitions.definition: To invoke damnation; to curse.
Generic intensifier. Fucking; bloody.
definitions.example: Shut the damn door!

definitions.definition: Very; extremely.
definitions.example: That car was going damn fast!

definitions.definition: Used to express anger, irritation, disappointment, annoyance, contempt or suprise, etc. See also dammit.
Given to wilful, perverse deviation from the expected norm; tending to stray
Obstinate, contrary and unpredictable
Not on target
To be, or act as, a grandfather to.
To retain discontinued laws or rules for (a thing, person or organization previously affected by them).
The parent of someone's parent
A unitary council.
A unitary matrix or operator.
Having the quality of oneness.
(of a system of government or administration) That concentrates power in a single body, rather than sharing it with more local bodies.
definitions.example: a unitary authority

definitions.definition: (of an algebra) That contains an identity element.
(of a matrix or operator) Whose inverse is equal to its adjoint.
The result or outcome of a cause.
definitions.example: The effect of the hurricane was a devastated landscape.

definitions.definition: Impression left on the mind; sensation produced.
Execution; performance; realization; operation.
An illusion produced by technical means (as in "special effect")
definitions.example: The effect of flying was most convincing.

definitions.definition: (sound engineering) An alteration, or device for producing an alteration, in sound after it has been produced by an instrument.
definitions.example: I just bought a couple of great effects.

definitions.definition: (etc.) A scientific phenomenon, usually named after its discoverer.
definitions.example: Doppler effect

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Belongings, usually as personal effects.
Consequence intended; purpose; meaning; general intent; with to.
Reality; actual meaning; fact, as distinguished from mere appearance.
Manifestation; expression; sign.
To make or bring about; to implement.
definitions.example: The best way to effect change is to work with existing stakeholders.

definitions.definition: To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.
To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
definitions.example: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

definitions.definition: The act of rewinding.
A button or other mechanism for rewinding.
definitions.example: I meant to pause the picture, but hit the rewind by mistake.

definitions.definition: To wind (something) again.
To wind (something) back, now especially of cassette or video tape, CD, DVD etc.; to go back on a video or audio recording.
To go back or think back to a previous moment or place, or a previous point in a discourse.

A device used to dampen the effects of such things as sound, light, or fluid. Specifically, a baffle is a surface which is placed inside an open area to inhibit direct motion from one part to another, without preventing motion altogether.
definitions.example: Tanker trucks use baffles to keep the liquids inside from sloshing around.

definitions.definition: An architectural feature designed to confuse enemies or make them vulnerable.
(coal mining) A lever for operating the throttle valve of a winding engine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: Elongated portion.
Connected sequence of people or things.
To practice an ability.
definitions.example: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.

definitions.definition: To teach and form (someone) by practice; to educate (someone).
definitions.example: You can't train a pig to write poetry.

definitions.definition: To improve one's fitness.
definitions.example: I trained with weights all winter.

definitions.definition: To proceed in sequence.
To move (a gun) laterally so that it points in a different direction.
definitions.example: The assassin had trained his gun on the minister.

definitions.definition: To encourage (a plant or branch) to grow in a particular direction or shape, usually by pruning and bending.
definitions.example: The vine had been trained over the pergola.

definitions.definition: To trace (a lode or any mineral appearance) to its head.
To create a trainer for; to apply cheats to (a game).
To draw along; to trail; to drag.
To draw by persuasion, artifice, or the like; to attract by stratagem; to entice; to allure.
A monocular optical instrument that magnifies distant objects, especially in astronomy.
Any instrument used in astronomy for observing distant objects (such as a radio telescope).
To extend or contract in the manner of a telescope.
To slide or pass one within another, after the manner of the sections of a small telescope or spyglass.
To come into collision, as railway cars, in such a manner that one runs into another.
A seat across a boat on which a rower may sit.
definitions.example: The fisherman sat on the aft thwart to row.

definitions.definition: A brace, perpendicular to the keel, that helps maintain the beam of a marine vessel against external water pressure and that may serve to support the rail.
definitions.example: A well-made dugout canoe rarely needs a thwart.

definitions.definition: An act of thwarting; something which thwarts; a hindrance, an obstacle.
To cause to fail; to frustrate, to prevent.
definitions.example: Our plans for a picnic were thwarted by the thunderstorm.

definitions.definition: To place (something) across (another thing); to position crosswise.
To hinder or obstruct by placing (something) in the way of; to block, to impede, to oppose.
To move (something) across or counter to; to cross.
definitions.example: An arrow thwarts the air.

definitions.definition: A board game for two players with each beginning with sixteen chess pieces moving according to fixed rules across a chessboard with the objective to checkmate the opposing king.
An electronic device for detecting sound, vibration, etc., such as one fitted to an electric guitar or record player.
A pickup truck.
(usually attributive) Impromptu or ad hoc, especially of sports games and teams made up of randomly selected players.
definitions.example: At lunch we had a game of pickup hockey.

definitions.definition: An instance of approaching someone and engaging in romantic flirtation and courting with the intent to pursue romance, a date, or a sexual encounter. See also pick-up line, pick-up joint, pickup artist.
definitions.example: Hey, thanks for the drink, but if this is a pickup, I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A person successfully approached in this manner for romance or sex.
In various games, the fielding or hitting of a ball just after it strikes the ground.
An item that can be picked up by the player, conferring some benefit or effect; a power-up.
The act of a challenging party or candidate winning an electoral district held by an incumbent party or candidate. See also gain
definitions.example: The returns from the election show Apple Party candidate Jane Doe has made a pickup in the district of City West defeating Orange Party Incumbent Joe Smith

definitions.definition: The act of answering a telephone.
A relatively minor shot filmed or recorded after the fact to augment previous footage.
The act of collecting and taking away something or someone, usually in a vehicle.
Very wealthy and comfortable surroundings.
Something desirable but expensive.
Something that is pleasant but not necessary in life.
Freedom provided by the cessation of activities.
Free time, time free from work or duties.
Time at one's command, free from engagement; convenient opportunity; hence, convenience; ease.
Any of the poisonous umbelliferous plants, of the genera
The poison obtained from these Conium and Cicuta plants.
Any of several coniferous trees, of the genus Tsuga, that grow in North America; the wood of such trees.
Affected by hunger; desiring of food; having a physical need for food.
definitions.example: My kids go to bed hungry every night because I haven't got any money.

definitions.definition: Causing hunger
Eager, having an avid desire (‘appetite’) for something.
Not rich or fertile; poor; barren; starved.
definitions.example: a hungry soil

definitions.definition: A structure of hexagonal cells made by bees primarily of wax, to hold their larvae and for storing the honey to feed the larvae and to feed themselves during winter.
(by extension) Any structure resembling a honeycomb.
definitions.example: The wood porch was a honeycomb of termite tunnels before we replaced it.

definitions.definition: Voids left in concrete resulting from failure of the mortar to effectively fill the spaces among coarse aggregate particles.
Manufactured material used manufacture light, stiff structural components using a sandwich design.
(solar cell) The texture of the surface of a solar cell, intended to increase its surface area and capture more sunlight.
A space-filling packing of polytopes in 3- or higher-dimensional space.
To riddle something with holes, especially in such a pattern.
definitions.example: Termites will honeycomb a porch made of untreated pine.

definitions.definition: One who bonks.
Mad; crazy.
Any of several fish species, principally groupers and similar-appearing fish
To cause to become numb (physically or emotionally).
definitions.example: The dentist gave me novocaine to numb my tooth before drilling, thank goodness.

definitions.definition: To cause (a feeling) to be less intense.
definitions.example: He turned to alcohol to numb his pain.

definitions.definition: To cause (the mind, faculties, etc.) to be less acute.
To become numb (especially physically).
An act by which something is numbed.
To clean with water.
definitions.example: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

definitions.definition: To move or erode by the force of water in motion.
definitions.example: Heavy rains wash a road or an embankment.

definitions.definition: To separate valuable material (such as gold) from worthless material by the action of flowing water.
To clean oneself with water.
definitions.example: I wash every morning after getting up.

definitions.definition: To cover with water or any liquid; to wet; to fall on and moisten.
definitions.example: Waves wash the shore.

definitions.definition: To move with a lapping or swashing sound; to lap or splash.
definitions.example: to hear the water washing

definitions.definition: To be eroded or carried away by the action of water.
To be cogent, convincing; to withstand critique.
To bear without injury the operation of being washed.
definitions.example: Some calicoes do not wash.

definitions.definition: To be wasted or worn away by the action of water, as by a running or overflowing stream, or by the dashing of the sea; said of road, a beach, etc.
To cover with a thin or watery coat of colour; to tint lightly and thinly.
To overlay with a thin coat of metal.
definitions.example: steel washed with silver

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
